properties.remark: HWY 100 IN CENTERVILLE REPORTED TO HAVE WATER OVER IT.
THE WEST FORK OF THE PINEY RIVER HAS RISEN OUT OF ITS BANKS AND INTO LOW LYING FIELDS A FEW MILES SOUTHWEST OF DICKSON.
TREES DOWN ALONG RALLY HILL ROAD AND CHESTNUT RIDGE ROAD IN MAURY COUNTY
AT 896 FLOYD RD.... PORTION OF A ROOF MISSING AND TREES DOWN IN THE AREA.

EF-0 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WINDS NEAR 85 MPH TOUCHED DOWN ALONG FLOYD ROAD JUST SOUTHWEST OF EAGLEVILLE. APPROXIMATELY 100 TREES WERE SNAPPED...AND A RESIDENCE SUFFERED ROOF AND WINDOW DAMAGE...ALONG THE APPROXIMATE 1.5 MILE DAMAGE PATH. MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH WAS NEAR 100 YARDS.
POSSIBLE TORNADO DAMAGE. ABANDONED HOUSE WITH ROOF DAMAGE AND TREES DOWN. 896 FLOYD RD. 2 M SOUTH OF EAGLEVILLE NEAR CHAPEL HILL RD.
A COUPLE OF TREES DOWN SCATTERED ACROSS THE COUNTY.
BUILDING DAMAGE REPORTED ON ROBY DRIVE IN ERIN. SEVERAL TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN IN THE AREA AS WELL

TELEVISION MEDIA REPORTED SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO THE LORETTA LYNN RANCH AND MUSEUM IN HURRICANE MILLS. SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS WERE DESTROYED AND THE LORETTA LYNN HOME HAD CONSIDERABLE ROOF DAMAGE. HUNDREDS OF TREES WERE UPROOTED. DAMAGE WAS ESTIMATED AT 100000 DOLLARS. POSSIBLE TORNADO.
EF-1 TORNADO SURVEYED NEAR HURRICANE MILLS BY NWS STORM SURVEY.
A COUPLE TREES DOWN IN WAVERLY ALONG HWY 230 AND HWY 13.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMS AN EF-2 TORNADO IN HICKMAN COUNTY. THE TORNADO BEGAN NEAR WOLF CREEK RD WEST OF THE COBLE COMMUNITY AND ENDED BETWEEN BEAVERDAM ROAD AND HWY 50 SPANNING ABOUT 3 MILES. PATH WIDTH 300 YARDS. DAMAGE CONSISTED OF SNAPPED TREES... ALMOST TOTAL ROOF LOSS AND WALL COLLAPSE TO A BRICK HOME... MOBILE HOME DESTROYED AND WRAPPED AROUND A TREE... THREE BARNS DEMOLISHED... AND AN RV FLIPPED. HUNDREDS OF TREES WERE ALSO SNAPPED OR UPROOTED.
AN EF-1 TORNADO WAS SURVEYED NORTH OF WAYNESBORO ON HARRIS RD.
ROOF WAS REPORTED OFF A HOUSE AND VEHICLE DAMAGE WAS ALSO REPORTED IN THE COBLE COMMUNITY.
AT LEAST 15 HOUSES DAMAGED....SOME DESTROYED ALONG HWY 438....FROM HWY 50 WEST TO THE PERRY COUNTY LINE
SEVERAL TREES AND POWER LINES DOWNED ALONG WITH ROOF DAMAGE TO HOMES ALONG STATE HIGHWAY 48.

NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM CONFIRMED THAT AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ALONG WHITE BLUFF RD IN SOUTHEAST DICKSON COUNTY AROUND 245 AM WHERE MINOR TREE AND STRUCTURAL DAMAGE OCCURRED. THIS TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST TO KINGSTON SPRINGS IN SOUTHERN CHEATHAM COUNTY WHERE DOZENS OF TREES WERE SNAPPED ALONG CRAGGIE HOPE RD.
TREES DOWN ALONG HWY 48 IN DICKSON COUNTY
TWO TREES DOWN AND AN OPEN AIR BUILDING DESTROYED.
TREES DOWN AND ROOF DAMAGE ALONG WALKER CEMETERY RD AND SHAHAN RD.
4 FOOT DIAMETER OAK TREE COMPLETELY GONE ALONG PINE BLUFF RD.

*** 1 INJ *** AN EF0 TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN IN ASHLAND CITY ALONG GLORIA CIRCLE AND STRATTON BLVD. SOME MINOR ROOF DAMAGE OCCURRED AND SEVERAL TREES WERE UPROOTED AND SNAPPED ALONG THE ONE QUARTER MILE TRACK BEFORE IT LIFTED.
POWER LINES DOWN IN KINGSTON SPRINGS...ALSO A FALLEN TREE REPORTED ON A BUILDING
A COUPLE OF TREES DOWN AND POWER OUTAGES IN THE SOUTHERN PORTION OF THE COUNTY ON MORRIS AND LONG BRANCH ROAD.

AN EF-0 TORNADO MOVED THROUGH THE JOELTON AREA. DAMAGE BEGAN ALONG HARPER RD WHERE LARGE TREES WERE UPROOTED. MAINLY TREE DAMAGE OCCURRED ALONG THE 3.8 MILE PATH THAT ENDED ALONG WILKINSON RD. ROOF DAMAGE AND DAMAGE TO A BARN WAS ALSO OBSERVED.

A NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND WIND DAMAGE ALONG PINEWOOD RD EAST OF WALKER HILL RD IN WESTERN WILLIAMSON COUNTY. ONE BARN WAS DAMAGED AND SEVERAL TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. WINDS ESTIMATED AROUND 75 MPH.
SEVERAL TREES DOWNED ACROSS THE COUNTY WITH POWER OUTAGES THROUGHOUT THE COUNTY WITH SOME STRUCTURE DAMAGE ESPECIALLY IN HOHENWALD ON RUSH BRANCH ROAD.

AN NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM CONFIRMED EF0 TORNADO DAMAGE NEAR THE TOWN OF JOELTON FROM THE JANUARY 30TH 2013 EVENT. THE PATH BEGAN ALONG HARPER ROAD SOUTH OF INTERSTATE 24 ABOUT 3 MILES NORTHWEST OF JOELTON WHERE 3 LARGE TREES WERE UPROOTED. THE PATH CONTINUED NORTHEAST ALONG BIDWELL ROAD WHERE MINOR ROOF DAMAGE OCCURRED TO A BARN AND HOUSE AS WELL AS SEVERAL TREES UPROOTED OR SNAPPED. THE PATH APPEARS TO END 4 MINUTES LATER AND ABOUT 4 MILES LATER ALONG WILKINSON ROAD ABOUT 2.5 MILES NORTHEAST OF

*** 1 INJ *** ROBERTSON COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT REPORTED A PATH OF DAMAGE FROM SOUTHWEST TO NORTHEAST ACROSS SOUTHERN ROBERTSON COUNTY. 20 HOMES AND SEVERAL BARNS SUSTAINED MODERATE STRUCTURAL DAMAGE. NUMEROUS TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN. 1 TREE FELL ON A HOME AND INJURED A WOMAN. POSSIBLE TORNADO.
TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN AND STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO SOME BARNS REPORTED ALONG OLD GREENBRIER PIKE
TREE ACROSS HWY 43. BUILDING COLLAPSE IN LAWRENCEBURG

*** 1 INJ *** AN EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ALONG PINSON SCHOOL ROAD AND TRAVELED ACROSS POSSUM TROT ROAD UPROOTING AND SNAPPING SEVERAL TREES. ONCE THE TORNADO CROSSED THE INTERSECTION OF HWY 76 AND GUM STATION ROAD MORE SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE OCCURRED TO NUMEROUS HOMES AND A BARN. MOST HOMES HAD MINOR ROOF DAMAGE WHILE A FEW HAD SUBSTANTIAL ROOF DAMAGE. HUNDREDS OF TREES WERE UPROOTED AND SNAPPED ALONG GUM STATION ROAD...BETHLEHEM ROAD AND BAGGETT ROAD. THE TORNADO CAUSED SMALL AREAS OF SNAPPED AND UPROOTED TREES
*** 1 FATAL *** MAN WAS REPORTEDLY KILLED TAKING SHELTER IN A SHED ON ABERNATHY ROAD.
NUMEROUS TREES AND POWER LINES DOWNED ACROSS THE COUNTY WITH ALSO SOME REPORTS OF ROOF DAMAGE ACROSS THE COUNTY TOO.
LARGE TREE AND POWER LINE DOWN IN WEST NASHVILLE ALONG FOUNDRY DR AND ROBERTSON RD.
NUMEROUS TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN BLOCKING ROADWAYS
NUMEROUS TREES WERE REPORTED DOWN IN FAIRVIEW.
TREE DOWN ACROSS JOCELYN HOLLOW RD.
AT 7720 COVINGTON ROAD...JUST OUTSIDE OF CROSS PLAINS...SEVERAL TREES WERE DOWNED ACROSS THE AREA WITH POWER LINES DOWNED AND A BARN DESTROYED.

*** 1 INJ *** AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NORTHEAST OF THE I-65 AND TRINITY LN INTERCHANGE AND MOVED EASTWARD BEFORE LIFTING IN INGLEWOOD NEAR MCGAVOCK PIKE. SEVERAL MOBILE HOMES WERE HEAVILY DAMAGED BY FALLEN TREES NEAR THE START OF THE PATH WITH ONE MINOR INJURY. DOZENS OF OTHER HOMES HAD MINOR ROOF AND EXTERIOR DAMAGE WITH NUMEROUS TREES...POWER LINES...AND FENCES BLOWN DOWN. PATH LENGTH 3.2 MI AND PATH WIDTH 75 YARDS. MAX WINDS OF 80 MPH.
EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHDOWN OCCURRED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF DICKERSON PIKE AND TRINITY LANE.

AN NWS STORM SURVEY NOTED SCATTERED DOWNBURST WIND DAMAGE UP TO 1 MILE SOUTH OF THE EF0 EAST NASHVILLE TORNADO. SCATTERED TREES...TREE LIMBS...AND FENCES WERE BLOWN DOWN. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED UP TO 70 MPH.

A NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND A ONE HALF MILE WIDE AREA OF WIND DAMAGE FROM AROUND 1 MI WEST OF DOWNTOWN FRANKLIN INTO THE DOWNTOWN AREA. SCATTERED TREES...POWER LINES...AND LIGHT POLES WERE BLOWN DOWN. WINDS ESTIMATED AROUND 70 MPH.

AN EF1 TORNADO CROSSED INTERSTATE 65 FROM ROBERTSON COUNTY AND CAUSED NUMEROUS SNAPPED AND UPROOTED TREES ONE MILE NORTH OF THE HWY 25 AND HWY 31 JUNCTION. THIS TORNADO WAS THE CONTINUATION OF THE ROBERSTON COUNTY EF1 TORNADO.

AN NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF0 TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN JUST EAST OF DOWNTOWN FRANKLIN. THE TORNADO DAMAGED FOUR BUSINESSES ALONG FORT GRANGER DRIVE AND EDDY LANE. PASSING JUST NORTH OF LIBERTY ELEMENTARY SCHOOL...THE TORNADO THEN CAUSED MINOR ROOF DAMAGE TO SEVERAL HOMES ALONG LIBERTY PIKE...CADET LANE...AND FLINTLOCK DRIVE BEFORE LIFTING NEAR HIGHWAY 397. NUMEROUS TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN ALONG THE PATH. PATH LENGTH 1.2 MILES AND PATH WIDTH 75 YARDS. MAX WINDS AROUND 75 MPH.
NUMEROUS TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN ACROSS THE COUNTY INCLUDING SEVERAL TREES ON HOMES IN THE CITY OF FRANKLIN.

AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF TYREE SPRINGS ROAD AND SANDY VALLEY ROAD DESTROYING TWO GARAGE BUILDINGS. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED...AND SOME HOMES HAD MINOR ROOF DAMAGE. AS THE TORNADO TRAVELED 3.5 MILES TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED UNTIL THE TORNADO LIFTED ALONG UPPER STATION CAMP ROAD.

A NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND A LARGE AREA OF SCATTERED WIND DAMAGE FROM MALLORY LANE TO WILSON PIKE. TWO SCHOOLS AND SEVERAL HOMES RECEIVED ROOF DAMAGE IN THE AREA AND SCATTERED TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN. WINDS ESTIMATED AROUND 70 MPH.
TREES UPROOTED AND BACK PORCH DAMAGED IN COTTONTOWN
REPORT OF BROKEN WINDOWS... DAMAGED BARN... AND WALNUT TREE UPROOTED
LARGE TREES DOWN IN GOODLETTSVILLE
FOUR HOMES WITH ROOF AND WINDOW DAMAGE. ONE HOME HAD ITS GARAGE BLOWN INTO THE ROAD. MANY TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED.
TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN AND BARNS BLOWN DOWN. LARGE STREET SIGN DOWN AND OTHER TREE DEBRIS ALONG CENTERPOINT RD.

AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF HWY 31 AND SHUTE LANE. NUMEROUS TREES WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED WITH SOME TREES FALLING ON HOMES. SEVERAL HOMES HAD MINOR ROOF DAMAGE. THE MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE OCCURRED IN THE FAIRVIEW PLANTATION AND BAYPOINT SUBDIVISIONS. THE TORNADO TRAVELED 10.3 MILES AND CONTINUED TO UPROOT AND SNAP TREES AND CAUSE MINOR ROOF DAMAGE ALONG THE PATH UNTIL IT LIFTED NEAR BLEDSOE CREEK STATE PARK.

A WEAK TORNADO BEGAN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF HIGHWAY 258 AND SANDY VALLEY ROAD...THEN CONTINUED EAST TO NEAR UPPER STATION CAMP CREEK ROAD. SOME TREES WERE KNOCKED DOWN...A BARN WAS DESTROYED...A GARAGE WAS DESTROYED... AND THREE HOMES SUFFERED MINOR ROOF DAMAGE.

NWS NASHVILLE PERSONNEL CONFIRMED AN EF2 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN MOUNT JULIET ON GLENWOOD DRIVE AND LIFTED NEAR COOKS CHURCH ROAD. NUMEROUS BUSINESSES RECEIVED MINOR TO MAJOR DAMAGE AROUND THE MOUNT JULIET RD AND LEBANON RD INTERSECTION. HEAVY DAMAGE OCCURRED TO THE NEARBY MOUNT JULIET BALLPARK. DOZENS OF TREES AND POWER LINES WERE BLOWN DOWN AND SEVERAL HOMES RECEIVED MINOR DAMAGE ALONG THE PATH. PATH LENGTH 4.6 MILES AND PATH WIDTH 150 YARDS.
AT FAIRVIEW PLANTATION REPORTS OF BARK RIPPED OFF TREES... TWISTED METAL DEBRI... AND TOP STORY TORN OFF OLD BRICK BUILDING
ALONG BROWNS LN AND WOODLAKE DR... REPORTS OF TREES DOWN... WINDOWS BROKEN AND ROOF DAMAGE.
EIGHTEEN WHEELER FLIPPED OVER.
ROOF MISSING ON HOUSE IN WHITE BLUFF

NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM CONFIRMED EF0 TORNADO DAMAGE IN THE FAIRVIEW PLANTATION AREA OF SOUTHWEST GALLATIN UP TO THE 109 INTERSECTION. MOST OF THE DAMAGE WAS A RESULT OF TREES BEING UPROOTED AND TWISTED.

NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM HAS DETERMINED THAT STRAIGHT LINE WINDS CAUSED THE DAMAGE ALONG NUBIA ROAD NORTHWEST OF WESTMORELAND. MAJORITY OF THE DAMAGE WAS ISOLATED TREES THAT WERE SNAPPED. A CARPORT WAS DAMAGED DUE TO THE LOSS OF THE ROOF.
TREE ON MOBILE HOME AND KNOCKED OFF FOUNDATION IN HOHENWALD
TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN ON LOCK 4 ROAD IN GALLATIN. ALSO DAMAGE REPORTED AT GALLATIN MARINA.

AN NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND A TWO MILE LONG BY ONE HALF MILE WIDE AREA OF WIND DAMAGE IN FAR SOUTHEAST WILLIAMSON COUNTY. A BARN WAS DAMAGED ON FLAT CREEK RD AND TWO HOMES HAD ROOF AND SIDING DAMAGE ON GLENN RD. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED ON COMSTOCK RD AND CROSS KEYS RD. WINDS ESTIMATED AROUND 80 MPH.

AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF HWY 25 AND HARSH LANE DIRECTLY IMPACTING THE APPLE MARKET WITH MINOR ROOF DAMAGE. AS THE TORNADO TRAVELED 1.0 MILES A FEW TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED UNTIL THE TORNADO LIFTED VERY NEAR THE SUMNER COUNTY RC FLIERS AREA.
TREES DOWN IN A LINE ON ALEXANDER ROAD
WOODS FERRY RD. BLOCKED BY TREES

AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 2 MILES SOUTHWEST OF BETHPAGE ALONG ALEXANDER LANE THEN MOVED EAST BEFORE LIFTING IN FAR NORTHWEST TROUSDALE COUNTY EAST OF GRAVEL HILL ROAD. SEVERAL HOMES AND OUTBUILDINGS RECEIVED MINOR ROOF DAMAGE AND NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED IN ALL DIRECTIONS ALONG THE PATH. ONE SMALL SHED WAS DESTROYED ON WOLF HILL ROAD. PATH LENGTH 5.9 MILES AND PATH WIDTH 50 YARDS. MAX WINDS OF 85 MPH.

INITIAL DAMAGE WAS NOTED ALONG MANN ROAD IN NORTHERN WILSON COUNTY WHERE NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED AND MINOR ROOF DAMAGE WAS NOTED. INTERMITTENT DAMAGE CONSISTING OF HUNDREDS OF SNAPPED AND UPROOTED TREES AND SOME PROPERTY DAMAGE CONTINUED NORTHEAST FOR OVER 10 MILES. THE LAST EVIDENCE OF DAMAGE WAS ALONG HWY 141 HARTSVILLE PIKE JUST NORTH OF RIADON ROAD. AN NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND TSTM WIND DAMAGE IN SMYRNA. AN APARTMENT COMPLEX HAD MINOR ROOF DAMAGE ON WEAKLEY LN AND A TREE FELL ON A HOME ON NISSAN DR. A BARN WAS DAMAGED BY A FALLEN TREE ON S LAMAR ROAD AND SEVERAL TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN ON HICKORY GROVE RD. WINDS ESTIMATED AROUND 70 MPH.

AN NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN WEST OF EAGLEVILLE BEFORE LIFTING NORTHEAST OF TOWN. TWO FARM BUILDINGS WERE DAMAGED ALONG HWY 11 AND A COUPLE OF HOMES HAD MINOR ROOF DAMAGE ALONG HWY 269. HEAVIEST DAMAGE WAS ALONG NORTH MAIN ST...ELM ST...AND OAK ST WHERE SEVERAL HOMES RECEIVED ROOF AND SIDING DAMAGE. TWO BARNS WERE DAMAGED ALONG SWAMP RD BEFORE THE TORNADO LIFTED. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. PATH LENGTH 4.6 MI AND PATH WIDTH 150 YDS. MAX WINDS OF 90 MPH.

AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 4 MILES EAST-SOUTHEAST OF BETHPAGE IN EASTERN SUMNER COUNTY BEFORE LIFTING IN FAR NORTHWEST TROUSDALE COUNTY. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED IN ALL DIRECTIONS ALONG WOLF HILL ROAD AND GRAVEL HILL ROAD AND ONE SMALL SHED WAS DESTROYED. PATH LENGTH AROUND 1 MILE AND PATH WIDTH 50 YARDS. MAX WINDS OF 80 MPH.

AN NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM HAS CONFIRMED EF0 TORNADO DAMAGE IN NORTHEAST MACON COUNTY ABOUT 7 MILES NORTHEAST OF LAFAYETTE. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ALONG OWENS RD. JUST NORTH OF HOLLAND RD. AND CONTINUED NORTHEAST FOR 1.5 MILES TO JUST EAST OF JIMTOWN RD. AND SOUTH OF HEADY RIDGE RD. AN OLDER HOME SUFFERED SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE...A BARN SUFFERED SIGNIFICANT ROOF LOSS...AND A FEW HUNDRED TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED ALONG THE 1.5 MILE PATH.

TWO FARM BUILDINGS WERE DAMAGED ALONG HWY 11 AND A COUPLE OF HOMES HAD MINOR ROOF DAMAGE ALONG HWY 269. OUTBUILDINGS WERE DAMAGED ON WHITE OAK RD. ONE BARN WAS DESTROYED AND OTHERS DAMAGED ON HWY 46. ONE HOME LOST ITS ROOF ON HILL RD AND ANOTHER MOBILE WAS COMPLETELY DESTROYED ON HALIBURTON RD. AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON MCKNIGHT ROAD EAST OF CULLEOKA WHERE SEVERAL TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN AND ONE MOBILE HOME HAD MINOR SIDING DAMAGE. A FEW MORE TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN ON OLD BRUSH CREEK ROAD. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED IN ALL DIRECTIONS ALONG SIDE ROADS NORTH OF OLD BRUSH CREEK ROAD. A FEW MORE TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN ON JOE REEVES ROAD AND AN OLD BARN LOST PART OF ITS ROOF. PATH LENGTH 3.7 MILES WITH PATH WIDTH 50 YARDS. MAX WINDS 80 MPH.
